description: Real-time collaborative drawing and chat web app. Friends connect to a shared room, draw on a canvas synced via Socket.IO, and chat.

userStories.title: Root + server/client package.json files
userStories.description: Create the three package.json files needed for the project. (1) Root package.json: name='collab-draw', type='module', private=true, scripts.dev = 'concurrently "node server/index.js" "npm run dev --prefix client"', devDependencies: concurrently ^8.2.0. (2) server/package.json: name='collab-draw-server', type='module', dependencies: express ^4, socket.io ^4, nanoid ^5. (3) client/package.json: name='collab-draw-client', type='module', dependencies: react ^18, react-dom ^18, socket.io-client ^4, devDependencies: vite ^5, @vitejs/plugin-react ^4, tailwindcss ^3, postcss ^8, autoprefixer ^10. Do NOT run npm install — just write the JSON files.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json exists at root with type:module and scripts.dev using concurrently
server/package.json exists with type:module and correct dependencies
client/package.json exists with type:module and correct dependencies

userStories.title: server/index.js (Express + health endpoint) + server/rooms.js
userStories.description: Create server/index.js using ES module syntax. Import express, http (createServer), and the Server class from socket.io. Create Express app, attach it to http server, attach Socket.IO to http server. Add GET /health route returning JSON {ok:true}. Start listening on port 3001. Log '[server] listening on :3001' on startup. Create server/rooms.js that exports a createRoomManager() function. The room manager must expose: addUser(roomId, socketId, nickname, color), removeUser(socketId), getRoom(roomId), getRoomForSocket(socketId), and getUsersArray(roomId). Internally use two Maps: rooms (roomId→{users:Map,chatHistory:[]}) and socketToRoom (socketId→roomId).
acceptanceCriteria: server/index.js uses ES module imports, no require()

userStories.title: room:join and disconnect socket handlers (server)
userStories.description: In server/index.js, add Socket.IO connection handler. On room:join {roomId, nickname}: (1) check if room has MAX_USERS=5 users — if full emit room:error {message:'Room is full'} and return; (2) assign color from palette ['#ef4444','#3b82f6','#22c55e','#f59e0b','#a855f7'] based on current room size; (3) call roomManager.addUser(roomId, socket.id, nickname, color); (4) socket.join(roomId); (5) emit room:joined to sender with {users: getUsersArray(roomId), chatHistory: room.chatHistory}; (6) broadcast room:user_joined {id:socket.id, nickname, color} to rest of room. On disconnect: look up roomId via getRoomForSocket, call removeUser(socket.id), broadcast room:user_left {id:socket.id, nickname} to room. Log format: console.log('[room:join]', roomId, nickname). Wrap all handlers in try/catch — on error log it, do not emit anything.
acceptanceCriteria: room:join handler checks MAX_USERS and emits room:error if full
Joining user receives room:joined with full user list
Disconnect triggers room:user_left broadcast to remaining users

userStories.title: draw:* event relay (server)
userStories.description: In server/index.js, add handlers for four draw events. For draw:stroke_start {x,y,color,brushSize,tool}: look up roomId for socket, broadcast to room excluding sender via socket.to(roomId).emit('draw:stroke_start', {userId:socket.id, x, y, color, brushSize, tool}). For draw:stroke_move {x,y}: broadcast {userId:socket.id, x, y}. For draw:stroke_end {}: broadcast {userId:socket.id}. For draw:clear {}: broadcast draw:clear to room excluding sender. All wrapped in try/catch. No logging for draw events.
acceptanceCriteria: draw:stroke_start relayed to others in room with userId added
draw:clear relayed to all others in room
Sender does not receive their own draw events

userStories.title: chat:message handler (server)
userStories.description: In server/index.js, add handler for chat:message {text}. Validate: text must be a non-empty string, max 500 chars — if invalid silently return. Look up roomId and user's nickname from room state. Create message: {id: nanoid(), userId: socket.id, nickname, text: text.trim(), timestamp: Date.now()}. Append to room.chatHistory. If chatHistory.length > MAX_MESSAGES (200), trim oldest: chatHistory.splice(0, chatHistory.length - MAX_MESSAGES). Broadcast chat:message (the full message object) to entire room io.to(roomId).emit(...). Wrap in try/catch.
acceptanceCriteria: chat:message broadcast to all users in room including sender
Message object has id (nanoid), userId, nickname, text, timestamp fields
Empty or non-string text is silently ignored

userStories.title: cursor:move relay (server)
userStories.description: In server/index.js, add handler for cursor:move {x, y}. Look up roomId via getRoomForSocket(socket.id). Look up user's nickname and color from room users Map. Broadcast cursor:move {userId: socket.id, nickname, color, x, y} to all other sockets in room via socket.to(roomId).emit(). Wrap in try/catch. No logging.
acceptanceCriteria: cursor:move received from one client relayed to others with userId/nickname/color added
Sender does NOT receive their own cursor events

userStories.title: Vite + React client scaffold
userStories.description: Create the client/ folder structure: (1) client/index.html with , lang='en', title='CollabDraw', a #root div, and
acceptanceCriteria: client/index.html has #root div and module script
client/src/App.jsx is a valid React component
npm install in client/ completes without errors
npx vite build in client/ succeeds without errors

userStories.title: Tailwind CSS configuration
userStories.description: Add Tailwind CSS to the client. (1) client/tailwind.config.js: export default { content: ['./index.html','./src/**/*.{jsx,js}'], theme:{extend:{}}, plugins:[] }. (2) client/postcss.config.js: export default { plugins: { tailwindcss: {}, autoprefixer: {} } }. (3) client/src/index.css: three lines — @tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities; — nothing else. (4) Verify client/src/main.jsx already imports './index.css'. Update App.jsx to temporarily use className='min-h-screen bg-gray-100 flex items-center justify-center' to verify Tailwind works. Run npm install from client/ if tailwindcss is not yet installed.
acceptanceCriteria: client/tailwind.config.js exists with correct content array
npx vite build in client/ succeeds

userStories.title: App.jsx session state and routing
userStories.description: Implement client/src/App.jsx with full session state using useState. State variables: sessionState (default 'join'), roomId (default ''), nickname (default ''), myColor (default ''), myId (default ''), users (default []), chatMessages (default []), joinError (default null). When sessionState === 'join', render <JoinScreen onJoin={handleJoin} joinError={joinError} />. When sessionState === 'room', render <Room socket={socket} roomId={roomId} nickname={nickname} myColor={myColor} myId={myId} users={users} chatMessages={chatMessages} />. handleJoin(roomId, nickname) sets state and triggers socket connection. Import JoinScreen and Room (create as empty placeholder files if needed). Use function declaration: export default function App().
acceptanceCriteria: App.jsx has all 8 state variables with useState

userStories.title: JoinScreen component
userStories.description: Create client/src/components/JoinScreen.jsx. Props: onJoin(roomId, nickname), joinError (string|null). Render a centered full-screen div (min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center bg-gray-50). Inside: a card div with a title 'CollabDraw', two labeled text inputs (Room ID, Nickname), a Join button. On form submit: if either field is empty do nothing; otherwise call onJoin(roomId.trim(), nickname.trim()). If joinError is non-null, show it in a red text paragraph above the button. Styled entirely with Tailwind. export default function JoinScreen({ onJoin, joinError }).
acceptanceCriteria: Form renders Room ID input, Nickname input, and Join button
Empty field submission does nothing (no onJoin call)
Valid submission calls onJoin with trimmed values
joinError displays as red text when prop is non-null

userStories.title: useSocket hook
userStories.description: Create client/src/hooks/useSocket.js. Named export: export function useSocket({ roomId, nickname, onJoined, onUserJoined, onUserLeft, onDrawEvent, onChatMessage, onCursorMove, onError }). Use useEffect with [roomId, nickname] as dependencies. Only connect if both roomId and nickname are truthy. Import io from 'socket.io-client'. Create socket: const socket = io(). Emit room:join {roomId, nickname} after connecting. Register event listeners: room:joined → onJoined, room:user_joined → onUserJoined, room:user_left → onUserLeft, draw:stroke_start/move/end/clear → onDrawEvent with event name + payload, chat:message → onChatMessage, cursor:move → onCursorMove, room:error → onError. Keep socket in a ref so it's stable. Return {socket: socketRef.current, isConnected}. Cleanup: socket.disconnect(). No console.log.
acceptanceCriteria: Socket only connects when both roomId and nickname are truthy
All room:*, draw:*, chat:message, cursor:move events registered

userStories.title: Connect App.jsx to useSocket + sounds
userStories.description: Update App.jsx to use the real useSocket hook. Call useSocket({ roomId, nickname, onJoined, onUserJoined, onUserLeft, onDrawEvent, onChatMessage, onCursorMove, onError }). Implement callbacks: onJoined({users, chatHistory, myColor, myId}) → setSessionState('room'), setUsers(users), setChatMessages(chatHistory), setMyColor(myColor), setMyId(socket.id); onUserJoined(user) → setUsers(prev => [...prev, user]) + playJoin(); onUserLeft({id}) → setUsers(prev => prev.filter(u => u.id !== id)) + playLeave(); onChatMessage(msg) → setChatMessages(prev => [...prev.slice(-199), msg]) + playMessage(); onError({message}) → setJoinError(message). Import {playJoin, playLeave, playMessage} from './utils/sounds'. Store draw events for DrawingCanvas via a ref or callback (onDrawEvent will be passed down). Pass socket to Room.
acceptanceCriteria: onJoined transitions to room state and sets user list

userStories.title: sounds.js (Web Audio API)
userStories.description: Create client/src/utils/sounds.js. No imports from node_modules. Export three named functions: export function playJoin() — plays a short ascending two-note tone (e.g., 440Hz then 550Hz, 80ms each) using AudioContext + OscillatorNode + GainNode. export function playLeave() — plays a short descending tone (e.g., 440Hz then 350Hz). export function playMessage() — plays a brief click (short 880Hz tone, 50ms). Each function: create AudioContext, create oscillator, connect to gain, connect to destination, set frequency, start, stop after duration. Wrap entire function in try{}catch(e){} so audio errors are always silent.
acceptanceCriteria: Each uses Web Audio API (no audio files)

userStories.title: useDrawing hook
userStories.description: Create client/src/hooks/useDrawing.js. Named export: export function useDrawing(). Internal state: tool('brush'), color('#000000'), brushSize(6), isDrawing(false). Keep activeStrokes Map (userId→{color,brushSize,tool}) in a ref for tracking remote strokes. Returns: {canvasRef (useRef), tool, color, brushSize, setTool, setColor, setBrushSize, startStroke(ctx,x,y), moveStroke(ctx,x,y), endStroke(ctx), clearCanvas(ctx), drawRemoteStart(ctx,{userId,x,y,color,brushSize,tool}), drawRemoteMove(ctx,{userId,x,y}), drawRemoteEnd(userId)}. startStroke: set isDrawing=true, store in activeStrokes for local ('local' key), beginPath, moveTo(x,y). moveStroke: if isDrawing, lineTo(x,y), stroke. Apply compositing before each stroke: source-over for brush, destination-out for eraser. clearCanvas: ctx.clearRect(0,0,CANVAS_WIDTH,CANVAS_HEIGHT). Remote functions mirror local but use the userId's stored stroke state.
acceptanceCriteria: useDrawing returns all specified functions and state
canvasRef is a useRef suitable for attaching to <canvas>
Remote draw functions track per-userId stroke state

userStories.title: DrawingCanvas component
userStories.description: Create client/src/components/DrawingCanvas.jsx. Props: socket, drawingHook (the returned object from useDrawing()). Render: <canvas ref={drawingHook.canvasRef} width={1280} height={720} className='block bg-white cursor-crosshair' style={{touchAction:'none'}} />. Mouse handlers (attach to canvas): onMouseDown: get ctx = canvas.getContext('2d'), call drawingHook.startStroke(ctx,x,y), emit draw:stroke_start {x,y,color:drawingHook.color, brushSize:drawingHook.brushSize, tool:drawingHook.tool}. onMouseMove: if isDrawing: throttle to 16ms via lastStrokeTime ref, call drawingHook.moveStroke(ctx,x,y), emit draw:stroke_move {x,y}. Always: throttle cursor to 50ms via lastCursorTime ref, emit cursor:move {x,y}. onMouseUp: call drawingHook.endStroke(ctx), emit draw:stroke_end {}. useEffect listening to socket draw:* events → drawingHook.drawRemote*(ctx,...). isDrawing tracked via ref.
acceptanceCriteria: mousedown starts stroke and emits draw:stroke_start
mousemove emits draw:stroke_move at max 60fps (16ms throttle)
Remote draw events render via drawRemote* functions

userStories.title: Toolbar component (color, brush size, eraser, clear)
userStories.description: Create client/src/components/Toolbar.jsx. Props: tool, color, brushSize, setTool, setColor, setBrushSize, onClear, socket. Render a horizontal toolbar: (1) label + color input type='color' value={color} onChange={e=>setColor(e.target.value)}; (2) label + range input min=2 max=40 value={brushSize} onChange={e=>setBrushSize(Number(e.target.value))}; (3) 'Brush' button — onClick setTool('brush'), visually active (ring-2 ring-blue-500) when tool==='brush'; (4) 'Eraser' button — onClick setTool('eraser'), active when tool==='eraser'; (5) 'Clear' button — onClick calls onClear() then socket.emit('draw:clear'). Style with Tailwind: flex gap-4 items-center p-2 bg-white border-b.
acceptanceCriteria: Brush size slider changes brush size (2-40)
Brush/Eraser buttons toggle tool with visual active state
Clear calls onClear and emits draw:clear via socket

userStories.title: ChatPanel component
userStories.description: Create client/src/components/ChatPanel.jsx. Props: socket, messages (array), nickname, myColor. Render a flex-col container with: (1) scrollable message list — use useRef+useEffect to auto-scroll to bottom whenever messages changes; each message renders nickname in user's color (inline style for dynamic color), message text, and timestamp (new Date(timestamp).toLocaleTimeString()); (2) input area at bottom — text input (max 500 chars) + Send button. On send: if text is empty do nothing; emit chat:message {text: input.trim()}; clear input. Enter key also triggers send. Tailwind styling. Function declaration: export default function ChatPanel({socket, messages, nickname, myColor}).
acceptanceCriteria: Message list auto-scrolls to bottom on new messages
Each message shows nickname in user's color, text, and formatted time
Send button and Enter key both send messages

userStories.title: UserList sidebar component
userStories.description: Create client/src/components/UserList.jsx. Props: users (array of {id, nickname, color}), myId (string). Render a vertical panel with: (1) heading showing 'N / 5 users' where N = users.length; (2) list of users — each showing a small colored circle dot (w-3 h-3 rounded-full inline-block mr-2, with style={{backgroundColor:user.color}}) and the nickname, plus ' (you)' appended if user.id === myId. Tailwind styling: bg-white border-l p-3 flex flex-col gap-2. Function declaration: export default function UserList({users, myId}).
acceptanceCriteria: Each user has a colored dot matching their color
Updates reactively when users prop changes

userStories.title: CursorOverlay component
userStories.description: Create client/src/components/CursorOverlay.jsx. Props: socket, myId. Internal state: cursors (object/Map mapping userId→{nickname,color,x,y,lastSeen}). useEffect: listen to socket cursor:move events — on event, update cursors state with {nickname,color,x,y,lastSeen:Date.now()}. Also run a setInterval every 500ms to remove cursors where Date.now()-lastSeen > 2000 (CURSOR_FADE_TIMEOUT). Cleanup both on unmount. Render: a div with position:absolute (or className='absolute'), top-0, left-0, width=1280, height=720, pointer-events:none, overflow:hidden. Inside: for each cursor (excluding myId), render a small label div with position:absolute, left=cursor.x, top=cursor.y, the nickname in the user's color, small text. Function declaration: export default function CursorOverlay({socket, myId}).
acceptanceCriteria: Renders cursor labels for all remote users at correct positions
Cursor labels disappear after 2000ms of inactivity
Overlay has pointer-events:none (does not block canvas)
Does not render current user's own cursor

userStories.title: Room.jsx layout (assembles all components)
userStories.description: Create client/src/components/Room.jsx. Props: socket, roomId, nickname, myColor, myId, users, chatMessages. Call useDrawing() inside this component. Compose the full room UI with Tailwind flex layout: outer div h-screen flex flex-col. Top bar: <Toolbar tool={...} color={...} brushSize={...} setTool={...} setColor={...} setBrushSize={...} onClear={()=>drawingHook.clearCanvas(ctx)} socket={socket} />. Main area: flex flex-row flex-1 overflow-hidden. Center: relative div containing <DrawingCanvas socket={socket} drawingHook={drawingHook} /> and <CursorOverlay socket={socket} myId={myId} /> stacked (overlay is absolute). Right sidebar: flex flex-col with <UserList users={users} myId={myId} /> on top and <ChatPanel socket={socket} messages={chatMessages} nickname={nickname} myColor={myColor} /> below. Import all components. Function declaration: export default function Room({...}).
acceptanceCriteria: Room renders Toolbar, DrawingCanvas, CursorOverlay, UserList, ChatPanel
CursorOverlay is absolutely positioned over canvas

userStories.title: npm install + dev server smoke test
userStories.description: Run npm install from three directories: (1) root (installs concurrently), (2) server/ (installs express, socket.io, nanoid), (3) client/ (installs all client deps). Fix any package.json errors, missing deps, or version conflicts found during install. Then verify: run node server/index.js and confirm it starts without errors, GET http://localhost:3001/health returns {ok:true}. Fix any server startup errors. Then run npm run dev from root and confirm both server and client start. Open browser at http://localhost:5173 and confirm JoinScreen renders without browser console errors. Fix any import errors, missing modules, or config issues found during this verification.
acceptanceCriteria: npm install succeeds in root, server/, and client/ without errors
node server/index.js starts and responds to GET /health with {ok:true}
npm run dev from root starts both server (3001) and client (5173)
No unhandled errors in browser console or server terminal

userStories.title: End-to-end integration: two users draw and chat
userStories.description: With npm run dev running, open two browser tabs both at http://localhost:5173. Tab 1: enter roomId='testroom', nickname='Alice', click Join. Tab 2: enter roomId='testroom', nickname='Bob', click Join. Verify: (1) both tabs show both users in UserList; (2) drawing strokes on Tab 1 appear on Tab 2 in real time; (3) typing and sending a chat message on Tab 1 appears on Tab 2; (4) moving mouse over canvas on Tab 1 shows a cursor label on Tab 2. Fix any remaining bugs in socket event wiring, canvas rendering, state synchronization, or component prop passing. Common issues to check: socket not passed to all components that need it, drawingHook.canvasRef not attached, onDrawEvent not wired through to DrawingCanvas, chat messages not rendering with correct color.
acceptanceCriteria: Two users can join the same room simultaneously
Both users see each other in UserList
Drawing on one tab syncs to the other in real time
Chat messages from one tab appear on the other
Cursor label for remote user appears on canvas
No unhandled errors in browser console or server terminal
